goal: Implement P1.1: make the orchestrate loop's pause button actually halt execution. The pause_requested flag exists on OrchestrateRun and the UI endpoint flips it, but the executor loop never checks it — users click Pause yet the run keeps consuming tasks and budget.

tasks.name: Scaffold minimal platform workspace
tasks.instruction: Create platform/ with app/__init__.py, app/main.py (bare FastAPI app), app/db.py (SQLAlchemy setup reading DATABASE_URL), app/models.py (OrchestrateRun with id, status, pause_requested, paused_at, resumed_at fields), app/api/__init__.py, app/api/pipeline.py (stub router), requirements.txt (fastapi==0.111.0, sqlalchemy==2.0.30, pytest, httpx, uvicorn, psycopg2-binary, jinja2), tests/__init__.py, tests/conftest.py (in-memory SQLite fixtures), tests/test_p1_pause.py (placeholder so collection succeeds).

acceptance_criteria.text: pytest --collect-only succeeds without import errors on tests/test_p1_pause.py

produces.scaffold: platform/ with OrchestrateRun model, db session, FastAPI app, test fixtures

tasks.name: Add _check_pause() helper and cooperative gate in orchestrate loop
tasks.instruction: In platform/app/api/pipeline.py add _check_pause(db, run_id) that reads OrchestrateRun fresh from DB; returns True when pause_requested=True, False when run_id is None or not found. Add cooperative gate in orchestrate loop stub: after each task call _check_pause; if True set run status=PAUSED and break. Write tests: test_check_pause_helper_detects_flag, test_check_pause_returns_false_for_null_run_id, test_check_constraint_accepts_paused.

acceptance_criteria.text: _check_pause(db, run_id) returns True when pause_requested=True in DB (fresh read)
Orchestrate loop sets status=PAUSED when flag is set

tasks.name: Add POST /orchestrate-runs/{id}/pause endpoint
tasks.instruction: In platform/app/api/pipeline.py add POST /orchestrate-runs/{run_id}/pause. For RUNNING: set pause_requested=True, paused_at=now(), return 200 {status: ok}. For terminal/already-paused: return 409. Write tests: test_pause_endpoint_sets_flag_for_running, test_pause_endpoint_rejects_terminal_runs.

acceptance_criteria.text: POST /pause returns 200 and sets pause_requested=True for RUNNING run

tasks.name: Add POST /orchestrate-runs/{id}/resume endpoint
tasks.instruction: In platform/app/api/pipeline.py add POST /orchestrate-runs/{run_id}/resume. For PAUSED: clear pause_requested, set status=RUNNING, resumed_at=now(), return 200. For RUNNING+pause_requested=True: clear flag only, return 200. For terminal: return 409. Write tests: test_resume_endpoint_respawns_paused_run, test_resume_endpoint_clears_flag_for_running, test_resume_endpoint_rejects_terminal.

acceptance_criteria.text: POST /resume on PAUSED run sets status=RUNNING and resumed_at
POST /resume on RUNNING+flagged clears pause_requested only

tasks.name: Expose pause fields in run status endpoint and render Resume in UI panel
tasks.instruction: In platform/app/api/pipeline.py add GET /orchestrate-runs/{run_id} returning {id, status, pause_requested, paused_at, resumed_at}. In platform/app/templates/_orchestrate_panel.html render Resume form button (POST to /resume) when status==PAUSED. Write tests: test_run_status_endpoint_exposes_pause_fields, test_orchestrate_panel_renders_paused_with_resume.

acceptance_criteria.text: UI panel renders Resume button when status=PAUSED
